records.ety: [OE. smeren, smerien, AS. smierwan, smyrwan, fr. smeoru fat, grease; akin to D. smeren, OHG. smirwen, G. schmieren, Icel. smyrja to anoint. See Smear, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Smeared (smērd); p. pr. & vb. n. Smearing.]

defs: To overspread with anything unctuous, viscous, or adhesive; to daub; as, to smear anything with oil.
To soil in any way; to contaminate; to pollute; to stain morally; as, to be smeared with infamy.
To smudge, blur, or render indistinct (writing, pictures, etc.).
to vilify (a person); to damage (a person's reputation), especially falsely or by unfair innuendo, and with malicious intent.

records.ety: [OE. smere,. smeoru fat, grease; akin to D. smeer, G. schmeer, OHG. smero, Icel. smjör, Sw. & Dan. smör butter, Goth. smarþr fatness, smarna dung; cf. Lith. smarsas fat. Cf. Smirch.]

defs: Hence, a spot made by, or as by, an unctuous or adhesive substance; a blot or blotch; a daub; a stain.
quotes: Slow broke the morn, All damp and rolling vapor, with no sun, But in its place a moving smear of light.
